attacking_style: Team does not follow conventional attacking patterns; prioritizes opportunistic stage hunting and protecting selected leaders rather than predictable GC attacks or sprint trains.
team_tactics: Flexible role allocation where former stars work as domestiques for designated leaders; strong anticipatory positioning in technical/dangerous zones 3-5km from finish; willingness to sacrifice team hierarchy for tactical objectives.
terrain_preference: Demonstrates competence across diverse terrain (TTT, Classics, Grand Tours) but shows particular strength in technical finishes requiring positioning and crash avoidance rather than pure climbing or sprinting.
vulnerability: Lack of dedicated sprint train infrastructure; relies on opportunistic approaches rather than controlled lead-outs, limiting options in pure bunch sprint scenarios.
key_patterns: Hierarchy inversion: former stars like Vanmarcke domestique for tactical picks (2 instances)
Proactive danger anticipation: front positioning 3+ km before technical/crash zones (2 instances)
Stage hunting over GC consolidation: chasing stages rather than defending minimal gaps (2 instances)
TTT competence when built around specialists despite non-GC team status (1 instance, 14:58 time)
Immediate bike sacrifice protocols to protect GC leaders under 3km rule (1 instance)
form_trajectory: positive: 2019 Flanders tactics showed sophisticated team commitment delivering Bettiol victory, evolving from 2018 reactive protection
